SEARCH
Package graph

Class TopicManagerSingleton.TopicManager

java.lang.Object
graph.TopicManagerSingleton.TopicManager
Enclosing class:
TopicManagerSingleton
public static class TopicManagerSingleton.TopicManager extends Object
The registry and factory directory that manages, queries, and clears all active Topic channels in the system.

Flyweight Factory Design Pattern

The TopicManager acts as a Flyweight design pattern factory. To ensure that only one unique Topic instance exists for any given channel name, topics are stored and managed inside a ConcurrentHashMap. Resolving topics via getTopic(String) uses computeIfAbsent to atomically retrieve an existing instance or construct a new one, ensuring thread safety and preventing duplicate topic instances.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    clear()
    Clears all mapped topics, ensuring subscribers and publishers are cleanly detached to avoid memory leaks (Cascading Clean).
    Topic
    getTopic(String name)
    Retrieves an existing Topic or creates a new one if it doesn't exist (Flyweight).
    Collection<Topic>
    getTopics()
    Returns a Collection of all currently managed Topics.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • getTopic

      public Topic getTopic(String name)
      Retrieves an existing Topic or creates a new one if it doesn't exist (Flyweight). computeIfAbsent guarantees that the operation is atomic and thread safe.
      Parameters:
      name - the unique descriptive name of the topic to retrieve or construct
      Returns:
      the managed topic instance
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the provided topic name is null or empty

    • getTopics

      public Collection<Topic> getTopics()
      Returns a Collection of all currently managed Topics.
      Returns:
      a view collection containing all registered topic instances

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Clears all mapped topics, ensuring subscribers and publishers are cleanly detached to avoid memory leaks (Cascading Clean).

      To prevent strong reference chains from keeping decommissioned topics or agents in memory, this method explicitly clears subscriber and publisher registration lists from each topic prior to clearing the internal directory map.
